exemplar.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #5: To what extent do you agree with the claim "all models are wrong, but some are useful" (attributed to George Box)? Discuss with reference to mathematics and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Consistent focus on the prescribed title with clear framing of the knowledge question and explicit links to Mathematics and the Natural Sciences (Criterion A).
Use of a broad range of concrete, relevant examples (Euclidean vs. non-Euclidean geometry, Bohr model, Taylor series, epidemiological models) supporting arguments (Criterion B).
Evidence of critical engagement through raising counterclaims and ethical considerations (Criterion D).
weaknesses: Uneven depth of analysis; several passages remain descriptive or end mid-thought without full exploration of assumptions or implications (affecting Criterion B).
Organizational disruptions, including incomplete sentences, abrupt transitions and an unfinished conclusion, undermine clarity (Criterion C).
Limited sustained evaluation of different points of view and underdeveloped links between some examples and the guiding knowledge question (Criterion D).

annotations.comment: The reflection on whether Euclidean geometry was ever âwrongâ is thought-provoking but lacks deeper analysis of underlying assumptions and their philosophical implications.
The sentence ending with âwhile most scientific models strive forâ is incomplete, disrupting the flow; ensure all paragraphs convey a full idea before moving on.
The discussion of âwrongâ versus âusefulâ models is insightful but would benefit from an explicit articulation of a focused TOK knowledge question to guide the analysis.
The essay clearly states the prescribed title, explicitly referencing mathematics and another AOK, which ensures immediate relevance and sustained focus on the task.
The introduction effectively frames models as simplifications of reality and poses a clear knowledge question that anchors the essayâs exploration.
The discussion of local versus global behavior would be stronger with explicit LaTeX notation for the series and a brief sketch of its convergence properties.
The transition question about accuracy and utility is promising but remains vague; specify how this question links to the Areas of Knowledge under examination.
Definitions of objectivity and subjectivity need concrete illustration; include an example from mathematics or the sciences to show how perspectives shape model construction.
The GPS technology example effectively links theory to application, showing how both Euclidean and non-Euclidean models coexist to produce useful outcomes.
The claim that theorems can lead to incorrect solutions when applied to reality is valid but requires a specific example to substantiate how logical models fail outside their axiomatic systems.
The shift from Euclidean to non-Euclidean geometry is a strong historical example that vividly demonstrates how changing axioms can redefine a field of knowledge.
The conclusion ends mid-sentence (âserves in the development ofâ); complete the final thought to ensure the essay closes with a coherent summary.
The Bohr atomic model is well chosen to illustrate how a simplified, âwrongâ model can still serve pedagogical and practical roles in the sciences.
The question about Bohrâs model as a foundation for quantum computing is interesting but needs elaboration on the specific mathematical concepts that bridge chemistry and computing.
The Taylor series introduction trails off; complete the example by specifying the function and point of expansion to maintain clarity and coherence.
The discussion of general relativityâs assumptions would benefit from outlining how the continuum hypothesis shapes predictions and where it might introduce bias.
The parallel between heliocentric validation and later confirmation of general relativity smartly connects natural sciences with historical development of models.
The example of the Taylor series for e^{â1/x^2} at x=0 demonstrates a sophisticated understanding of where local expansions can misrepresent global behavior.
Linking the Taylor series to aerodynamics vividly shows how mathematical approximations can drive engineering innovation in defined contexts.
The claim that general relativity fails under quantum conditions is accurate but needs a brief mention of candidate quantum gravity theories to deepen the critique.
The COVID-19 epidemiological model example is timely but would be strengthened by citing specific statistics or studies to quantify the modelâs errors and impacts.
Recommending holistic data sets is a good start; propose specific strategies such as stratified sampling or fairness-aware algorithms to mitigate biases.
The discussion of AI bias in medical models insightfully raises ethical concerns about data representativeness and the illusion of precision.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
